역대상 15:1.content: David made himself houses in David’s city; and he prepared a place for God’s ark, and pitched a tent for it.

역대상 15:2.content: Then David said, “No one ought to carry God’s ark but the Levites. For Yahweh has chosen them to carry God’s ark, and to minister to him forever.”

역대상 15:3.content: David assembled all Israel at Jerusalem, to bring up Yahweh’s ark to its place, which he had prepared for it.

역대상 15:4.content: David gathered together the sons of Aaron and the Levites:

역대상 15:5.content: of the sons of Kohath, Uriel the chief, and his brothers one hundred twenty;

역대상 15:6.content: of the sons of Merari, Asaiah the chief, and his brothers two hundred twenty;

역대상 15:7.content: of the sons of Gershom, Joel the chief, and his brothers one hundred thirty;

역대상 15:8.content: of the sons of Elizaphan, Shemaiah the chief, and his brothers two hundred;

역대상 15:9.content: of the sons of Hebron, Eliel the chief, and his brothers eighty;

역대상 15:10.content: of the sons of Uzziel, Amminadab the chief, and his brothers one hundred twelve.

역대상 15:11.content: David called for Zadok and Abiathar the priests, and for the Levites, for Uriel, Asaiah, Joel, Shemaiah, Eliel, and Amminadab,

역대상 15:12.content: and said to them, “You are the heads of the fathers’ households of the Levites. Sanctify yourselves, both you and your brothers, that you may bring the ark of Yahweh, the God of Israel, up to the place that I have prepared for it.

역대상 15:13.content: For because you didn’t carry it at first, Yahweh our God broke out in anger against us, because we didn’t seek him according to the ordinance.”

역대상 15:14.content: So the priests and the Levites sanctified themselves to bring up the ark of Yahweh, the God of Israel.

역대상 15:15.content: The children of the Levites bore God’s ark on their shoulders with its poles, as Moses commanded according to Yahweh’s word.

역대상 15:16.content: David spoke to the chief of the Levites to appoint their brothers as singers with instruments of music, stringed instruments, harps, and cymbals, sounding aloud and lifting up their voices with joy.

역대상 15:17.content: So the Levites appointed Heman the son of Joel; and of his brothers, Asaph the son of Berechiah; and of the sons of Merari their brothers, Ethan the son of Kushaiah;

역대상 15:18.content: and with them their brothers of the second rank, Zechariah, Ben, Jaaziel, Shemiramoth, Jehiel, Unni, Eliab, Benaiah, Maaseiah, Mattithiah, Eliphelehu, Mikneiah, Obed-Edom, and Jeiel, the doorkeepers.

역대상 15:19.content: So the singers, Heman, Asaph, and Ethan, were given cymbals of bronze to sound aloud;

역대상 15:20.content: and Zechariah, Aziel, Shemiramoth, Jehiel, Unni, Eliab, Maaseiah, and Benaiah, with stringed instruments set to Alamoth;

역대상 15:21.content: and Mattithiah, Eliphelehu, Mikneiah, Obed-Edom, Jeiel, and Azaziah, with harps tuned to the eight-stringed lyre, to lead.

역대상 15:22.content: Chenaniah, chief of the Levites, was over the singing. He taught the singers, because he was skillful.

역대상 15:23.content: Berechiah and Elkanah were doorkeepers for the ark.

역대상 15:24.content: Shebaniah, Joshaphat, Nethanel, Amasai, Zechariah, Benaiah, and Eliezer, the priests, blew the trumpets before God’s ark; and Obed-Edom and Jehiah were doorkeepers for the ark.

역대상 15:25.content: So David, the elders of Israel, and the captains over thousands, went to bring the ark of Yahweh’s covenant up out of the house of Obed-Edom with joy.

역대상 15:26.content: When God helped the Levites who bore the ark of Yahweh’s covenant, they sacrificed seven bulls and seven rams.

역대상 15:27.content: David was clothed with a robe of fine linen, as were all the Levites who bore the ark, the singers, and Chenaniah the choir master with the singers; and David had an ephod of linen on him.

역대상 15:28.content: Thus all Israel brought the ark of Yahweh’s covenant up with shouting, with sound of the cornet, with trumpets, and with cymbals, sounding aloud with stringed instruments and harps.

역대상 15:29.content: As the ark of Yahweh’s covenant came to David’s city, Michal the daughter of Saul looked out at the window, and saw king David dancing and playing; and she despised him in her heart.
